metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit deployment sequencing note (Jira IDEA workflow must be configured before or atomically with code deployment) AND specify a rollback/back-out path (e.g., revert code if Jira workflow cannot be updated, or add a soft-failure mode for missing Jira statuses) to satisfy T4's rollback/expand-contract requirement.
findings.finding: The plan defers fixing the reconciler's abort-on-transition-failure bug (explicitly out of scope) while simultaneously introducing a new Jira status `IDEA` that requires a Jira-side prerequisite. If the Jira IDEA workflow status is not configured before the code is deployed, any `idea` ticket entering the reconciler will cause a Jira transition failure, which — per the unpatched bug — aborts the entire reconciler pass mid-way, silently skipping mutations for all subsequent tickets. The plan provides no deployment sequencing (configure Jira first, then deploy), no graceful-degradation fallback (e.g., treat missing IDEA as a soft warning), and no rollback path for this scenario.
findings.location: Context section — 'Out of scope' paragraph; Success Criteria — Jira round-trip bullet; Child story 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' child story, add a pre-condition gate: the mapping layer should emit a logged warning (not an exception) when the remote Jira project does not expose an IDEA transition, leaving the local status unchanged rather than aborting. Document this as the graceful-degradation contract. Separately, note in the parent plan that EITHER the reconciler bug must be fixed in the same release OR this soft-failure mode must be implemented before shipping.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether a Jira `IDEA` workflow state must be provisioned by a Jira admin before the code ships, specify the exact prerequisite steps (project key, transition graph, who is responsible), and document the safe interim reconciler behavior for `idea` tickets before the Jira state exists (e.g., treat `idea` as an `excluded_status` in `OutboundDiffConfig` until IDEA is live).
findings.finding: The plan asserts `idea` maps to a Jira status `IDEA` but does not verify that `IDEA` exists (or will be created) as a reachable workflow state in the connected Jira project, nor does it specify the concrete admin prerequisite. The codebase documents the live DIG workflow as having only {To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done} (config.py:28, outbound_fields.py:95-102). The reconcile.py preflight gate (line 194) raises `StatusMappingError` and aborts the entire reconciler pass when an outbound mutation references a status absent from `local_to_jira_status` — so if `IDEA` is not yet provisioned in Jira when the first `idea`-status ticket is synced, every subsequent reconciler pass will abort.
findings.location: Success Criteria / child story 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping'
findings.suggested_fix: In the child story 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping', add an explicit prerequisite checklist item: '[ ] Jira admin has created an IDEA workflow status in project <KEY> reachable from To Do, before the code is merged to main.' Also add `idea` to `OutboundDiffConfig.excluded_statuses` (defaulting to `{"archived", "deleted", "idea"}`) so that reconciler passes do not abort while the Jira workflow state is being provisioned — then remove the exclusion once IDEA is confirmed live.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In the 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' child story, spell out the workflow-transition prerequisite explicitly: a Jira project administrator must add `IDEA` as a status (and the necessary transitions from 'To Do' and to 'Done') in the DIG project workflow before the mapping can be tested or certified — and confirm or pre-arrange this action before committing to the story's AC.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` 'subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite,' but never specifies that this prerequisite requires a Jira project administrator to add an `IDEA` status to the DIG project workflow — an external action outside the codebase. The live DIG workflow is documented in the codebase as having only {To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done}; `blocked` and `cancelled` could not map directly and required annotation-label workarounds. If `IDEA` does not exist in the Jira workflow when the Jira child story ships, every `idea`-status ticket will cause `transition_issue_by_name` to raise a RuntimeError (no transition reaches 'IDEA') and hard-abort the reconciler pass.
findings.location: Child story 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' / Success Criteria: '`idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the child story description: 'Prerequisite: a Jira project administrator must add `IDEA` as a workflow status in the DIG project (transitions required: any → IDEA on create, IDEA → Done on close) before this story's AC can be verified against a live Jira instance.' If that admin action cannot be confirmed before implementation begins, route this child story through a short SPIKE to verify the Jira workflow is configurable as needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] The 'status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion' child story must specify that `idea` is added to `common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_status` first, then `types.py` is regenerated via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`, because `types.py` is a generated artifact with a CI drift-gate.
findings.finding: The `TicketStatus` enum is auto-generated from `common.schema.json` via `gen_types.py` with an enforced CI drift-gate, but the plan never names `common.schema.json` as the authoritative source that must be updated first — it only says to add `idea` to the 'status vocabulary'. An executor who edits `types.py` directly will be blocked by the drift-gate at CI time with no clear recovery path described.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the child story's file-impact list: `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (the source of truth for the enum) and note the required regeneration command `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` as a build step before running the drift-gate check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate one child (or a separate integration step) as the owner of running the full test suite against the combined changes and add a corresponding AC.
findings.finding: No child is designated as the integration owner for the parent AC 'make lint and the full test suite pass on the integrated result'. Each child only asserts per-child `make lint` passes; no child verifies that the combined changes pass the full suite.
findings.location: parent AC — absent from entire sibling roster
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 81b4 (the foundational child whose merge unblocks all others) an AC: '[ ] The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated.' Alternatively, add a post-merge integration step to the definition of done for the parent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] The '`idea → closed` gate bypass' child story must specify exactly where the `from-status == idea` early-return is inserted: either as a guard in `close_ticket()` between the open-children check and the `_completion_precheck` call, or via a new `current_status` parameter threaded into `_completion_precheck` — and must include `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` in its file-impact list.
findings.finding: `_completion_precheck` in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` receives `ticket_type` but NOT `current_status` (the from-status). The plan's 'idea → closed gate bypass' child story says the bypass is keyed on `from-status == idea`, but the function whose signature does not carry that information would need to be restructured or the bypass logic placed elsewhere. The plan does not specify where the from-status check is inserted or how the function signature changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Place the bypass as `if current_status == 'idea': return (result without precheck)` in `close_ticket()` immediately after the open-children guard block (lines 279-299), before the `_completion_precheck` call at line 311 — this keeps the structural guard unconditional and adds zero parameters to the existing function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to child 81b4 (or a designated sibling) asserting that `rebar search` / the search API returns `idea` tickets and does not exclude them by status.
findings.finding: No child covers the 'search' surface guarantee from parent success criterion 2, which explicitly states idea tickets should 'still appear in list and search'. Child 81b4 covers list; no child mentions search behavior or adds a test-verified AC for it.
findings.location: child 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 (scope/AC) — absent from entire sibling roster
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 81b4's Acceptance Criteria: '[ ] `rebar search <term>` returns `idea` tickets matching the term (test-verified).' and add a scope note for whichever file(s) implement the search filter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'depends on child 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 being merged first' note to the scope or Why section of children eced, 1982, 2918, and 6173.
findings.finding: Child 81b4 is an undeclared prerequisite for children eced, 1982, 2918, and 6173 — the 'idea' TicketStatus literal must exist before any other child can function correctly — but no child documents this ordering dependency, creating a concurrency merge risk.
findings.suggested_fix: In each of the four dependent children, add a dependency line in the scope section, e.g.: 'Prerequisite: child 81b4-985e-b3e8-4026 (TicketStatus literal update) must be merged before this story is implemented.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand child 81b4's CLAUDE.md AC to explicitly require lifecycle documentation: close-bypass behavior, validate exemptions, and the Jira IDEA mapping prerequisite.
findings.finding: Child 81b4's AC says 'The committed CLAUDE.md describes the idea status', but the parent AC requires 'The committed CLAUDE.md documents the idea status and its lifecycle.' The lifecycle behaviors — close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira mapping quirks — are each owned by separate children that have no scope item for updating CLAUDE.md, leaving lifecycle documentation potentially incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace child 81b4's AC item '[ ] The committed CLAUDE.md describes the idea status.' with: '[ ] The committed CLAUDE.md documents the idea status, its lifecycle (eligible transitions, close-bypass behavior, validate exemptions), and the Jira IDEA mapping prerequisite.'
Alternatively, add a CLAUDE.md scope item to each relevant child (eced, 1982, 6173) to contribute their section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-to-two sentence positive rationale in the Context or Approach section explaining why a first-class status is chosen over tag-based or boolean alternatives — specifically that a status provides structural unclaimability (a tagged `open` ticket remains claimable via `claim <id>` without the tag filter, whereas an `idea`-status ticket is never `open` and thus never reachable by `claim_core`'s `open`-status precondition).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing a first-class status over the most obvious structural alternative: a tag-based parking lot (e.g., tagging `open` tickets `idea` and adding `--without-tag=idea` to `ready`/`next-batch`/`validate` filters). The Context section explains what `idea` does but not why a new status is the right abstraction over tagging or a separate boolean `draft` field.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Context: 'A tag on an `open` ticket would require patching every claim/dispatch surface to filter by tag and would still leave a window where a newly created ticket is `open` before the tag is applied; a status is the only mechanism that makes the ticket structurally unclaimed from genesis — `claim_core` checks for `open` status, so an `idea`-status ticket is unreachable by the claim primitive without any additional filtering.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the AC set — e.g., a dogfooding note ('a user can capture, list, and promote an idea end-to-end on the CLI without it appearing in ready/next-batch') or a before/after workflow comparison — so the user-facing improvement is verifiably observable beyond internal test passage.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism (dogfooding session, before/after workflow comparison, or operational metric) is specified to confirm that the improvement is felt by actual users of the tool, as opposed to passing only internal technical invariants.
findings.suggested_fix: Append an AC such as: 'An end-to-end smoke test or dogfooding step demonstrates: (1) `idea create` produces a ticket not visible in `ready` or `next-batch`; (2) `idea → open` promotes it to claimable work; (3) `idea → closed` succeeds without a completion gate error.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify WHERE the Jira workflow-transition prerequisite is documented (e.g., a JIRA_SETUP.md section, a project README block, or an inline note) and state the minimum prerequisite (e.g., 'the Jira project must have a custom status IDEA in the workflow before any transition will succeed').
findings.finding: The Jira child story AC states round-trip is 'subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite' but does not identify WHERE that documentation lives or WHAT the prerequisite is, leaving an implementing agent unable to verify or satisfy it without stopping to search.
findings.location: Success Criteria — '`idea` round-trips to Jira `IDEA` (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'Prerequisite: the target Jira project must have a workflow status named IDEA configured by a Jira admin; see `docs/jira-setup.md` for instructions. The parity test mocks Jira responses and passes without the live prerequisite; live smoke-testing requires it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'certified' in the AC section (e.g., 'certified = the child story ticket carries the `certified` status in this system, indicating code review and CI both passed') so each of the five AC lines is self-contained and verifiable without external knowledge.
findings.finding: The five AC lines 'Child story X is closed and certified' use the term 'certified' without defining it; an agent cannot determine what observable condition constitutes certification or how to verify it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five 'Child story … is closed and certified' lines
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'closed and certified' with a precise observable condition, e.g.: 'Child story "status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion" ticket status is `certified` (as defined in the project workflow) and its CI is green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign the CLAUDE.md `idea` documentation update to a specific child story (e.g., 'status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion') so AC6 has a corresponding described deliverable.
findings.finding: AC6 requires a committed CLAUDE.md update documenting the `idea` status and lifecycle, but no child story or described deliverable in the plan body is assigned responsibility for this work — it is an orphan criterion with no described work covering it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6; Decomposition (five child stories)
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'updates CLAUDE.md to document the `idea` status and its lifecycle' to the file-impact list of the 'status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion' child story, since that story already owns the vocabulary definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either define the 'workflow-transition prerequisite' in the plan body (e.g., as a setup step or test fixture requirement) and reference it in AC4, or remove the qualifier from the Success Criteria if it is not a real constraint.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria reference a 'documented workflow-transition prerequisite' for the Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` round-trip, but this prerequisite is never described as a deliverable, defined in the plan body, or covered by any AC — leaving the qualifier dangling.
findings.location: Success Criteria — Jira round-trip bullet; AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific document that describes the Jira workflow-transition prerequisite (create it if it doesn't exist, e.g., a `docs/jira-setup.md` or a section in CLAUDE.md) and add updating/creating it to the AC for the 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' child story.
findings.finding: The plan references 'the documented workflow-transition prerequisite' for the Jira IDEA mapping but does not identify which document this refers to, whether it already exists, or whether it will be created/updated as part of this work. The AC only mandates CLAUDE.md coverage of the `idea` status lifecycle; it does not cover Jira setup instructions.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'idea round-trips to Jira IDEA (subject to the documented workflow-transition prerequisite)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line to the Jira child story: 'A `docs/jira-setup.md` (or equivalent) section describes how to add the IDEA status to a Jira project workflow, and CLAUDE.md cross-references it.' This resolves both the stale-reference risk and the T4 operational-sequencing gap.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `validate` exemption approach filters `idea` tickets in `normalize_issues` (or equivalent upstream filter) so they are excluded from ALL scored checks — especially `check_orphaned_tasks` (WARNING-severity, scored) and `check_ticket_count` — not only `check_empty_epics` (verbose-only, never scored); update the plan description to name the correct scored checks.
findings.finding: The plan claims 'empty-epic' findings contribute to the validate score for idea tickets and frames exempting idea from that check as preventing score pollution. In the actual codebase, `check_empty_epics` (validate_checks.py:91-137) emits ONLY `verbose`-severity findings, which are never counted by `calculate_score` (`_ALWAYS_SHOWN` excludes `verbose`). The score impact of idea tickets in validate comes from `check_ticket_count` (counts all non-closed non-error tickets), `check_orphaned_tasks` (fires on parentless non-epic/non-bug tickets at WARNING severity, which IS scored), `check_missing_descriptions`, and `check_interface_contracts`. The implementation approach (filtering `idea` from `normalize_issues`) would correctly handle all of these, but the plan's description of the mechanism mislabels `check_empty_epics` as the key scored concern, which could mislead the implementer into a narrower fix.
findings.location: Plan section: '`validate` noise exemptions'; validate_checks.py:91-137 (`check_empty_epics`); validate.py:136-186 (`normalize_issues`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the validate exemption child story, explicitly target `normalize_issues` in `_engine_support/validate.py` by adding `idea` to the status exclusion set (alongside `closed`/`error`/`fsck_needed`). Note that `check_empty_epics` is already verbose-only and does not affect the score; the plan should name `check_orphaned_tasks` and `check_ticket_count` as the primary scored concerns.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (`process_create`) as a required file-impact in the 'status vocabulary + dispatch exclusion' child story and specify that `process_create` must be extended to read `data.get('status')` and set `state['status']` to the genesis value when a non-default status is provided on CREATE.
findings.finding: `process_create` in `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` never reads a `status` field from the CREATE event's data payload — initial status is hardcoded to `"open"` in `make_initial_state()`. The plan's 'atomic idea command' child story relies on a single genesis CREATE event setting `status=idea`, but the reducer processor that must be modified to honour a `status` field on CREATE is never named in any child story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] The 'Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping' child story must enumerate by name the exact status-map constants that need updating (e.g. `config.local_to_jira_status`, `config.jira_to_local_status`, `inbound_differ._JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS`, `inbound_translate._LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES`) and specify which of these the parity test covers.
findings.finding: The Jira child story says 'five status maps + parity test' but the plan never names which five map constants require updating. The codebase has at least four known status-map constants across three files: `local_to_jira_status` and `jira_to_local_status` in `config.py`; `_JIRA_TO_LOCAL_STATUS` and `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL` in `inbound_differ.py`; and `_LOCAL_STATUS_VALUES` and `_REBAR_STATUS_LABEL_TO_LOCAL` in `inbound_translate.py`. The discrepancy between 'five' and the discoverable set makes the parity-test scope ambiguous.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
